heroSubtitle: Organize documents in channel trees, parse with AI, and build RAG-ready knowledge bases. One platform for your team's knowledge.
painDesc: We built openKMS because teams struggle with:
pain1Text: Documents, PDFs, and notes live in silos—email, shared drives, wikis—making it hard to find what you need.
pain2Text: PDFs and images stay as blobs. No way to search, extract, or link content across your organization.
pain3Title: Manual work that should be automated
pain3Text: Repetitive document parsing, layout extraction, and indexing drain time and introduce errors.
ben1Text: Organize everything in channel trees (like Google Drive). Upload PDF, images, HTML—AI parses and converts to searchable Markdown.
ben2Text: Build knowledge bases from your documents. Ask questions and get answers grounded in your own content.
ben3Text: OIDC SSO or local accounts, fine-grained roles, and a console for permissions, data security, and platform settings.
funcKb2: Semantic search over chunks and FAQs (pgvector when configured)
funcOnt1: Datasets mapped from PostgreSQL data sources
funcOnt2: Object types and link types for your domain model
funcOnt3: Optional Neo4j indexing and graph exploration
funcPipe1: Configurable document pipelines and command templates
funcPipe2: Async parsing jobs; per-channel pipeline and auto-process
funcPipe3: Models linked to pipelines for OCR / VL / extraction
ctaHint: Sign in with your account to use the full application.
